title: Hedera echinops
intro: Hedera echinops, also known as the English ivy or Celtic ivy, is a perennial plant in the family Araliaceae, native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its glossy, dark green leaves and woody stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce stress, and boost the immune system. Bioactive constituents of Hedera echinops include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs, to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Hedera echinops is a species of evergreen climber native to the Mediterranean region.
